mnemonics.title: GREEN IT Pillars
mnemonics.content:
ENERGY: Energy-efficient hardware and practices
RECYCLING: E-waste proper disposal
EXTEND: Lifecycle management
GREEN: Use of renewable energy
NORMAL: Normalize virtualization
INNOVATE: Cloud and modern tech
THINK: Sustainable design principles

acronyms.usage: Centralized desktop hosting for energy efficiency

visualTips.visualization: Think of PUE as a fraction: Total Energy (numerator) / IT Energy (denominator). Draw a pie chart showing IT energy vs facility overhead (cooling, lighting).
Imagine one large pizza (physical server) being sliced into multiple smaller pizzas (virtual machines), each serving different customers.
Draw arrows from power source → UPS → PDU → Servers → Cooling. Highlight where energy is lost as heat at each step.
Side-by-side images: Thin client (small box, 5-15W) vs Desktop tower (large box, 50-200W) showing power difference.

analogies.analogy: Like a multiplex theater showing multiple movies on one building (physical server), each screen (virtual machine) running different content independently.
Like fuel efficiency in a car: lower PUE (like higher MPG) means less waste for the same output.
Like toxic waste from factories, e-waste is poison in our electronics that must be specially handled to prevent environmental contamination.
Like a shared taxi service versus everyone owning a car - shared resources reduce total vehicles (servers) needed.
Like a car engine automatically idling down when stopped at a traffic light, saving fuel.
